---
title: "Add-on: Values Missing from Main Analysis"
subtitle: "Reliability, SEM global fit, Odds Ratio, Outcome mapping, Faircloth comparison"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    number-sections: true
    df-print: kable
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
  cache: true
---

<!--
ZWECK
  Diese Chunks erzeugen die Werte, die im Manuskript stehen, aber in den
  gerenderten QMDs NICHT ausgegeben werden:
    (A) Reliabilität (omega / composite reliability) und AVE je Konstrukt
    (B) SEM-GLOBALFIT (CFI/TLI/RMSEA/SRMR) je Modell × Outcome  -> bisher nur R2
    (C) Donor-Status: Odds Ratio + 95% CI + Pseudo-R2  (Quelle der OR = 2.707)
    (D) Outcome-Mapping-Check (welche Variable = "last" vs "annual")
    (E) Korrekte Behandlung des Faircloth-"Nested"-Vergleichs

VORAUSSETZUNG
  Diese Chunks NACH den bestehenden Chunks einfügen, in denen
  `cfa_specs`, `sem_specs`, `DATASETS`, `params` bereits definiert sind.
  Sie re-fitten die Modelle eigenständig mit lavaan, hängen sich aber an
  deine vorhandenen Spezifikationen an. Variablennamen ggf. an deine
  params anpassen (Kommentare markieren die Stellen mit  ## ANPASSEN).
-->

```{r addon-setup}
#| cache: false
# --- Module sources (portable, from repo root via here::here) ----------------
source(here::here("R", "00_paths.R"))       # DATA_DIR, CSV_DIR, OUT_DIR etc.
source(here::here("R", "01_packages.R"))    # library() calls
source(here::here("R", "11_fit_helpers.R")) # coerce_sem_data, SEM_REGISTRY etc.
source(here::here("R", "12_lavaan_models.R")) # cfa_*/sem_* builders
source(here::here("R", "20_fit_cfa.R"))     # fit_cfa(), tidy_fit(), reliability_ave()
source(here::here("R", "21_fit_sem.R"))     # fit_sem(), sem_paths_r2()
source(here::here("R", "40_tables.R"))      # save_table(), gt_fit_summary()
source(here::here("R", "10_datasets.R"))    # DATASETS = list(fc, bo, ro, cross)
stopifnot(exists("DATASETS"))

# Re-create cfa_specs and sem_specs (mirrors 01_main_analysis.qmd)
# Edit params$* here if needed to match your render parameters.
params <- list(
  primary_estimator         = "MLR",
  ordinal_estimator         = "WLSMV",
  primary_amount_outcome    = "OF02_02_num_log",
  secondary_amount_outcome  = "OF02_01_num_log",
  binary_outcome            = "OF_Spender",
  ses_mode                  = "none"
)

cfa_specs <- list(
  fc_higher = list(syntax = cfa_fc_higher_order_original(), data = DATASETS$fc),
  fc_coreB  = list(syntax = cfa_fc_core_B(),                data = DATASETS$fc),
  bo        = list(syntax = cfa_bo_original(),              data = DATASETS$bo),
  ro        = list(syntax = cfa_ro_original(),              data = DATASETS$ro)
)

sem_specs <- list(
  fc = list(builder = sem_fc_original, data = DATASETS$fc),
  bo = list(builder = sem_bo_original, data = DATASETS$bo),
  ro = list(builder = sem_ro_original, data = DATASETS$ro)
)

# Helper: extract lavaan object from wrapper
as_lavaan <- function(x) {
  if (inherits(x, "lavaan")) return(x)
  if (!is.null(x$fit)    && inherits(x$fit, "lavaan"))    return(x$fit)
  if (!is.null(x$lavaan) && inherits(x$lavaan, "lavaan")) return(x$lavaan)
  if (!is.null(x$model)  && inherits(x$model, "lavaan"))  return(x$model)
  x
}

pick <- function(fm, nm) if (nm %in% names(fm)) unname(fm[[nm]]) else NA_real_
```

## (A) Reliabilität (omega) und AVE je Konstrukt

```{r addon-reliability}
#| cache: true
reliability_tbl <- purrr::imap_dfr(cfa_specs, function(s, id) {
  fit <- as_lavaan(fit_cfa(s$syntax, s$data,
                           estimator = params$ordinal_estimator, model_id = id))

  # omega = composite reliability (kongenerisch, tau-equivalence nicht erzwungen)
  om  <- tryCatch(semTools::compRelSEM(fit, tau.eq = FALSE, return.total = FALSE),
                  error = function(e) tryCatch(semTools::reliability(fit)["omega", ],
                                               error = function(e2) NA_real_))
  ave <- tryCatch(semTools::AVE(fit),
                  error = function(e) setNames(rep(NA_real_, length(om)), names(om)))

  tibble::tibble(model     = id,
                 construct = names(om),
                 omega     = round(as.numeric(om), 3),
                 AVE       = round(as.numeric(ave[names(om)]), 3),
                 AVE_ok    = ifelse(!is.na(AVE), AVE >= .50, NA))
})

save_table(reliability_tbl, "paper_table_reliability")   # nutzt deinen Helper
gt::gt(reliability_tbl) |> gt::tab_header("Reliability (omega) and AVE by construct")
# ERWARTUNG: Werte zwischen ~.50 und ~.95. Hier wirst du sehen, ob "Brand Scale"
# (FC_BS) tatsaechlich kollabiert -> dann CR/omega niedrig, AVE < .50.
# Die Manuskript-Zahlen .0608/.0785/.0923 muessen .608/.785/.923 o.ae. heissen.
```

## (B) SEM-Globalfit je Modell × Outcome (bisher nur R²)

```{r addon-sem-fit}
#| cache: true
outcomes_cont <- c(params$primary_amount_outcome, params$secondary_amount_outcome)

sem_fit_tbl <- purrr::imap_dfr(sem_specs, function(s, id) {
  purrr::map_dfr(outcomes_cont, function(oc) {
    fit <- as_lavaan(fit_sem(s$builder, s$data, outcome = oc,
                             ses_mode = params$ses_mode))   ## ANPASSEN falls arg anders heisst
    fm  <- lavaan::fitMeasures(fit)
    tibble::tibble(
      model  = id, outcome = oc,
      n      = lavaan::lavInspect(fit, "ntotal"),
      # robuste -> skalierte -> naive Variante, je nach Verfuegbarkeit (MLR)
      CFI    = dplyr::coalesce(pick(fm,"cfi.robust"),  pick(fm,"cfi.scaled"),  pick(fm,"cfi")),
      TLI    = dplyr::coalesce(pick(fm,"tli.robust"),  pick(fm,"tli.scaled"),  pick(fm,"tli")),
      RMSEA  = dplyr::coalesce(pick(fm,"rmsea.robust"),pick(fm,"rmsea.scaled"),pick(fm,"rmsea")),
      SRMR   = pick(fm,"srmr")
    )
  })
}) |> dplyr::mutate(across(c(CFI,TLI,RMSEA,SRMR), \(x) round(x, 3)))

save_table(sem_fit_tbl, "paper_table_sem_fit")
gt::gt(sem_fit_tbl) |> gt::tab_header("Structural model global fit (CFI/TLI/RMSEA/SRMR)")
# Damit ersetzt du die im Artikel genannten, nicht auffindbaren Strukturfit-Werte
# (Boenigk .988/.976, Faircloth .830/.854, Romero .847/.800) durch echte Zahlen.
```

## (C) Donor-Status: Odds Ratio + 95% CI + Pseudo-R²

```{r addon-donor-or}
#| cache: true
# Brand-Equity-Score je Architektur aus dem Messmodell, dann logistische
# Regression auf Spenderstatus. Das ist die transparente Quelle fuer ein OR.
# (Im SEM/probit-WLSMV gibt es keine direkte OR -> dort kommt Pseudo-R2/probit.)
donor_or_tbl <- purrr::imap_dfr(cfa_specs, function(s, id) {
  fit <- as_lavaan(fit_cfa(s$syntax, s$data,
                           estimator = params$ordinal_estimator, model_id = id))
  fs  <- as.data.frame(lavaan::lavPredict(fit))

  # Ein Gesamt-BE-Score: higher-order Faktor falls vorhanden, sonst Mittel der
  # standardisierten First-Order-Faktoren.
  be_col <- intersect(c("BE","BO_BE","FC_BE","RO_BE"), colnames(fs))
  be <- if (length(be_col)) fs[[be_col[1]]] else rowMeans(scale(fs))

  d <- s$data
  d$be_z  <- as.numeric(scale(be))
  d$donor <- as.integer(as.character(d[[params$binary_outcome]]))  ## ANPASSEN: OF_Spender_bin (0/1)

  m0 <- glm(donor ~ 1,            data = d, family = binomial())
  m1 <- glm(donor ~ be_z + SES_z, data = d, family = binomial())   ## ANPASSEN: SES-Variable

  or <- exp(coef(m1)["be_z"])
  ci <- tryCatch(exp(confint(m1)["be_z", ]),
                 error = function(e) exp(coef(m1)["be_z"] + c(-1,1)*1.96*summary(m1)$coef["be_z","Std. Error"]))
  mcfadden  <- 1 - as.numeric(logLik(m1) / logLik(m0))
  n_full    <- stats::nobs(m1)
  nagelk    <- (1 - exp((m1$deviance - m0$deviance)/n_full)) / (1 - exp(-m0$deviance/n_full))

  tibble::tibble(model = id,
                 OR = round(or,3), OR_lo = round(ci[1],3), OR_hi = round(ci[2],3),
                 McFadden_R2 = round(mcfadden,3), Nagelkerke_R2 = round(nagelk,3),
                 n = n_full)
})

save_table(donor_or_tbl, "paper_table_donor_or")
gt::gt(donor_or_tbl) |> gt::tab_header("Donor status: OR (per +1 SD brand equity) + pseudo-R2")
# WICHTIG: OR hier = pro +1 SD Brand-Equity-Score. Pruefe, ob deine
# urspruengliche OR = 2.707 ebenfalls pro SD oder pro Roheinheit war.
```

## (D) Outcome-Mapping-Check: welche Variable ist "last" vs "annual"?

```{r addon-outcome-map}
# Klaert die Beschriftung von Tabelle 7. Im Code ist OF02_02 = primary,
# OF02_01 = secondary -- aber welche ist "letzte Spende" und welche "Jahressumme"?
purrr::walk(c("OF02_01_num_log","OF02_02_num_log"), function(v) {
  x <- DATASETS$bo[[v]]
  cat(sprintf("%-18s  n=%d  mean(log)=%.2f  median(log)=%.2f  range=[%.2f, %.2f]\n",
              v, sum(!is.na(x)), mean(x,na.rm=TRUE), median(x,na.rm=TRUE),
              min(x,na.rm=TRUE), max(x,na.rm=TRUE)))
})
# Faustregel: die Jahressumme ist im Mittel groesser als die letzte Einzelspende.
# Danach die R2-Spalten in Tabelle 7 korrekt benennen.
```

## (E) Faircloth-"Nested"-Vergleich – korrekt statt erfunden

```{r addon-nested-correct}
#| cache: true
# Das Manuskript behauptet Model A "collapse" (CFI .0673) -> Model B (CFI .0943),
# Delta-Chi2 = 3481.4, Delta df = 71. Im Output ist fc_higher CFI = .956 (guter Fit)
# und fc_coreB CFI = .977. Ausserdem: A (df=221) und B (df=40) haben UNTERSCHIEDLICHE
# Indikatorsets -> sie sind NICHT genestet -> ein Chi2-Differenztest ist unzulaessig.
# Korrekt: beide Fits deskriptiv berichten und ueber AIC/BIC vergleichen.
fcA <- as_lavaan(fit_cfa(cfa_specs$fc_higher$syntax, cfa_specs$fc_higher$data,
                         estimator = params$ordinal_estimator, model_id = "fc_higher"))
fcB <- as_lavaan(fit_cfa(cfa_specs$fc_coreB$syntax,  cfa_specs$fc_coreB$data,
                         estimator = params$ordinal_estimator, model_id = "fc_coreB"))

nested_tbl <- tibble::tibble(
  model = c("Model A (full attribute set)", "Model B (core, attributes removed)"),
  CFI   = round(c(pick(lavaan::fitMeasures(fcA),"cfi.scaled"), pick(lavaan::fitMeasures(fcB),"cfi.scaled")),3),
  RMSEA = round(c(pick(lavaan::fitMeasures(fcA),"rmsea.scaled"), pick(lavaan::fitMeasures(fcB),"rmsea.scaled")),3),
  SRMR  = round(c(pick(lavaan::fitMeasures(fcA),"srmr"), pick(lavaan::fitMeasures(fcB),"srmr")),3),
  AIC   = tryCatch(c(AIC(fcA), AIC(fcB)), error=function(e) c(NA,NA)),
  BIC   = tryCatch(c(BIC(fcA), BIC(fcB)), error=function(e) c(NA,NA))
)
gt::gt(nested_tbl) |> gt::tab_header("Faircloth: descriptive fit comparison (NOT a nested chi-square test)")
# Nur falls A und B WIRKLICH genestet sind (identische Items, B = Restriktion von A),
# waere folgendes zulaessig:
# lavaan::lavTestLRT(fcB, fcA, method = "satorra.bentler.2010")
```
